title: podman ps reports incorrect exit status
body:
`podman ps` shows `Exited (125)` no matter what the actual exit status:

```
# for i in 0 1 2;do podman run registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7/rhel:latest bash -c "exit $i";echo $?;done
0
1
2
# podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE                                          COMMAND          CREATED AT                      STATUS                        PORTS   NAMES
0d3d5b16464e   registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7/rhel:latest   bash -c exit 0   2018-02-26 20:41:35 +0000 UTC   Exited (125) 16 seconds ago           vibrant_swirles
5b0d6cbcf9d3   registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7/rhel:latest   bash -c exit 2   2018-02-26 20:41:36 +0000 UTC   Exited (125) 15 seconds ago           keen_agnesi
f61b6c4674e9   registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7/rhel:latest   bash -c exit 1   2018-02-26 20:41:36 +0000 UTC   Exited (125) 16 seconds ago           hopeful_ptolemy
```

podman-0.2.2-1.git525e3b1.fc27

comments.body: Well that's properly interesting... We're definitely retrieving the proper exit code at some point.
`ps` and `run` both use the same function to obtain exit codes, which should be stored in the DB, and only set once. It's very concerning that they're changing after first accessed.
